channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canadian Animators Earn Oscars, Advocate for Streaming Support of Local Arts
item.description: Canadian animators had a remarkable showing at the Oscars this year, marking a significant achievement for the nation's film industry. 'The Girl Who Cried Pearls,' a Canadian animated short, won the O...

item.title: Australian and global markets react to Iran conflict as oil prices rise
item.description: The Australian sharemarket dropped on Monday as tensions in the Middle East continued to unsettle global markets, with oil prices climbing steadily. The S&P/ASX 200 fell 36.1 points, or 0.4 per cent, ...

item.title: Sindh High Court annuls federal ombudsperson’s decision in National Savings officer’s harassment case
item.description: The Sindh High Court (SHC), Hyderabad circuit, has nullified an earlier order issued by the Federal Ombudsman Secretariat for Protection Against Harassment of Women in the Workplace against a senior o...

item.title: Pakistan urges coordinated global response to rising Islamophobia
item.description: On the International Day to Combat Islamophobia, Pakistan’s Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar urged the world to go beyond symbolic condemnations and tackle the deep-rooted structures that enable anti-Muslim...

item.title: Arizona Man Requests Death Penalty After Confessing to Pastor's Murder
item.description: Adam Sheafe, a 51-year-old man from Arizona, has requested the death penalty after admitting to murdering Pastor William Schonemann, 76, in April 2025. Sheafe, who is representing himself in court, ha...

item.title: How NMG Ownership Change Can Strengthen Regional Climate Communication
item.description: After COP30, it became clear that fighting climate change requires action not only in governments and labs but also through strong public communication. The recent acquisition of a controlling stake i...

item.title: Michael B. Jordan Gains Strong Momentum for Best Actor Oscar with 'Sinners'
item.description: American actor Michael B. Jordan is making a strong push for the Academy Award for Best Actor for his performance in the film 'Sinners,' directed by Ryan Coogler. The movie, a supernatural horror set ...

item.title: French Envoys Rejected by Kremlin in Attempt to Include EU in Ukraine Peace Talks
item.description: In February, French national security advisers Emmanuel Bonne and Bertrand Buchwalter traveled to Moscow to advocate for the inclusion of European countries supporting Kyiv in potential peace negotiat...

item.title: Kenyan Police Commanders Develop Plan to Prevent Election Campaign Violence
item.description: Police commanders across Kenya's counties have come together to draw up a solid plan aimed at curbing violence during political campaigns. This move comes as the country gears up for the 2027 General ...

item.title: Fire in SCB Medical College ICU, Cuttack, results in multiple fatalities
item.description: In the early hours of Monday, March 16, 2026, a tragic fire broke out in the intensive care unit of SCB Medical College Hospital in Cuttack, Odisha, resulting in the deaths of at least ten patients. F...

item.title: UK government to unveil £50m support plan for households facing rising heating oil prices
item.description: The UK government is preparing a £50 million support plan to assist households affected by the sharp rise in heating oil prices following the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, which has driven crude oil ...

item.title: Nigerian Navy Destroys Illegal Refinery in Rivers, Seizes Stolen Fuel in Cross River
item.description: The Nigerian Navy don intensify their operation against crude oil theft and illegal bunkering. Dem destroy one illegal refinery wey don start again for Okolomade Community, Abua-Odual LGA, Rivers Stat...

item.title: Delta State Approves Funding For Police HQs, Road Projects, Hostels, And Social Interventions
item.description: The Delta State Government, under Governor Sheriff Oborevwori, don approve N34 billion for construction of 25 Divisional Police Headquarters across all Local Government Areas as dem dey prepare for po...

item.title: How Media Shapes Public Perception of Corporate Decisions
item.description: In Tanzania, decisions made in company boardrooms often take on a different meaning when they reach the public through media coverage. Boards focus on long-term planning, governance, and legal respons...

item.title: Rappler+ Launches First Town Hall Featuring Investigative Reports on Chinese Espionage
item.description: Rappler is launching quarterly Rappler+ Town Halls to foster community engagement among its supporters and promote independent journalism. The first event is scheduled on March 27, 2026, at the Rapple...

item.title: High Gas Prices Force Family to Cancel Trip, Highlighting Economic Strain
item.description: Rising gas prices across the United States, driven by ongoing conflict in Iran and resulting disruptions to global oil supply, have forced many American families to make difficult financial decisions....

item.title: US Allies Face Increased Responsibility in Strait of Hormuz Amid Trump’s Iran Actions
item.description: Since the beginning of the Trump administration, the US has pushed its allies to contribute more to global security, often with a mix of bluster and criticism. Following strikes on Iran, President Tru...

item.title: Complete List of 2026 Oscar Nominees and Winners
item.description: The 2026 Oscars saw 'Sinners' leading with 16 nominations, followed closely by 'One Battle After Another' with 13. Other notable films with multiple nods include 'Marty Supreme,' 'Frankenstein,' and '...

item.title: UConn Leads as Top Seed in 2026 Women’s NCAA Tournament Bracket
item.description: The 2026 Women’s NCAA Tournament bracket has been announced, with UConn securing the No. 1 overall seed as they aim for an undefeated season and their 13th national title. Led by key players Sarah Str...

item.title: UK Announces £2.5 Billion Investment to Develop Nuclear Fusion Technology
item.description: The UK government has announced a major initiative to establish a nuclear fusion sector, committing £2.5 billion over five years. This plan includes constructing one of the world’s first prototype fus...
